article_meta.title: Why a nation of 1.45 billion wants more children16 December 2024ShareSaveSoutik BiswasIndia correspondentShareSaveAFPLas

reasoning: The model makes some good identifications but also has issues. The first two items (Tamil Nadu 'exaggerated noises' and 'But guess what?') are genuine author-prose bias correctly identified. The third item flags 'In other words, Mr Goli says, "India is getting old before getting rich"' — this is attributed to Mr Goli and is essentially a quote, so flagging it is an attribution rule violation. The model misses some other potential bias instances but catches the most notable ones. The bias type labels for the first two are reasonable though debatable.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: The third flagged item ('In other words, Mr Goli says, "India is getting old before getting rich"') is clearly attributed to Mr Goli — the author uses 'Mr Goli says' and puts the key phrase in quotes. This is an attribution rule violation, though the model does acknowledge it's attributed in the explanation. The first two items are correctly from author prose.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: The first two items are defensible author-level bias. The third item is questionable due to attribution. So 1 out of 3 items is problematic.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model catches the most notable instances ('exaggerated noises', 'guess what'). It could have also flagged 'nudged past China' as Word Choice (though that's more minor), or the framing around 'aggressive family welfare programmes' and 'unintended consequence' as author editorializing, or the characterization of RSS as 'Hindu nationalist' and 'ideological backbone' which carries connotation. Some missed but the biggest ones were caught.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: The 'Subjective Qualifying Adjectives' label for 'exaggerated noises' is reasonable — could also be Word Choice. 'Sensationalism/Emotionalism' for 'But guess what?' is defensible though could also be Spin or Word Choice. The labels are close enough to be acceptable though not perfect.
